metadata.system_prompt:
You have joined the share house as a new resident. DaVinci, Donatello, Michelangelo, and Raffaello are members of the share house. I will provide you with the previous exchanges from the conversation. Here, "A" refers to your reply. Please carefully observe the tone, attitude, values, and other cues. For example, pay attention to the following points:
    - which first-person pronoun you uses,
    - whether you tend to be concise or prefer to speak in a detailed and polite manner,
    - whether you use casual or formal language,
    - how long you usually make your responses,
    - how you use punctuation.
    Especially, please pay attention to the length of your responses.
    Based on these observations, please predict your next response by imitating your communication style.
metadata.user_prompt:
previous interactions :
    train_day
    Please predict your response to the following message:question

scores.message: Haha, friend, since we’re talking about fairness and sharing, how about the fridge space? It’s always a bit tricky with fresh ingredients and packaged foods piling up! What if we create a weekly rotation for storage priorities so everyone gets their fair share? I’m all ears—got any awesome ideas on how to make this work smoothly for all our hungry tummies?
scores.response: All of us don't cook everyday right?
scores.prediction:
You have joined the share house as a new resident. DaVinci, Donatello, Michelangelo, and Raffaello are members of the share house. I will provide you with the previous exchanges from the conversation. Here, "A" refers to your reply. Please carefully observe the tone, attitude, values, and other cues. For example, pay attention to the following points:
    - which first-person pronoun you uses,
    - whether you tend to be concise or prefer to speak in a detailed and polite manner,
    - whether you use casual or formal language,
    - how long you usually make your responses,
    - how you use punctuation.
    Especially, please pay attention to the length of your responses.
    Based on these observations, please predict your next response by imitating your communication style.
    <｜User｜>previous interactions :
    Q(DaVinci): Haha, friend! Imagine you just won 100 million—no limits, no worries! What wild and bold adventure would you dive into first? Don’t hold back, let your biggest dreams fly!
A: Well, I'll travel all around the world because I can do it only while I'm physically healthy and young.

Q(Donatello): Very exciting plan! Now, let's analyze this from a practical standpoint: if you had 100 million, how would you realistically divide it among investment, living expenses during travel, and savings for future needs?
A: Thanks! I'll spend 50 million to travel fee and spend rest of them for saving. I don't know well about investment, therefore afraid of failure.

Q(Michelangelo): Wow, your dream to travel the world while young and healthy is truly inspiring! I’m curious—does this dream come from a clear, goal-driven plan to explore specific places and experiences, or is it more about following your feelings and values, embracing whatever adventure life brings?
A: Thanks for the advice, I'll consider and collect information about low-risk investments for my future. Answering your question, I'm just following my blurred dream to have irreplacable experience.

Q(Raffaello): Oh... speaking of journeys and dreams, I must confess a slight concern. I noticed that the shared refrigerator space is becoming rather cramped due to many packaged foods taking up room. I'm not being picky, but fresh ingredients do require some breathing space to maintain their quality. I suppose, like in art and life, preserving a certain quality of environment benefits us all, does it not? How do you feel about balancing convenience with that shared standard of living we all appreciate?
A: The decrease of shared space is a big problem, don't you think so? We need to take balance of convenience and living quality  in order to maintain comfortable and respectful share house lives.

Q(DaVinci): Haha, friend, the fridge space really does need some mutual concession to keep harmony in our kitchen masterpiece! How about we shake things up a bit and rearrange the layout so fresh ingredients get their breathing room? What’s your brilliant idea for reorganizing the fridge space to keep it both practical and beautiful? Share your creative plan!
A: Why don't we reduce own own spaces and spe

Q(DaVinci): Haha, friend, I think you meant "reduce own spaces and spe..." maybe "space"? That’s a cool thought! Are you suggesting we all gently minimize our personal fridge spots to create more shared space for fresh ingredients? That sounds awesome! Balancing our own needs and the community's is super important! So, what do you think about setting a time for everyone to chat and agree on how much space each can use? It’ll be fun and fair! Your ideas will make our fridge a masterpiece of harmony! What do you say, friend?
A: I totally agree with you. So when should we discuss our spaces?

Q(DaVinci): Haha, friend, speaking of cool projects, guess what? Tonight we’re starting a super fun 'dream room' design challenge! Donatello will dive into the technical wizardry to make sure everything works perfectly, and Michelangelo will bring in those artistic vibes to make it look amazing! So, what wild and wonderful ideas do you have to make our dream room totally awesome? Come on, friend, let your creativity run wild! Haha!
A: Dream room sounds fantastic! Let me see, I'll  bring something everyone can play with, such as card games and board games, what do you think of my plan?

Q(Donatello): Your game idea definitely sparks excitement! Now, from a practical angle, we need to consider space utilization, material cost, and maintenance to ensure the dream room is both fun and functional. In your view, should we prioritize practicality or design aesthetics to get the best balance?
A: I think we should prioritize practicality

Q(Michelangelo): Wow, your thoughtful balance between practicality and design really sparks my creative fire! You know, art and innovation always need that brave heart to break conventions and push limits. So, when crafting something as special as our dream room, would you be ready to face some tough challenges and hardships to reach perfect harmony, or would you lean towards an easier path that still feels good enough?
A: To make our ideal dream room come true, don't hesitate to confront difficulties.

Q(Raffaello): Ah... speaking of our creative pursuits, I must confess last night’s atmosphere was quite... spirited. I intended to focus on my artwork, yet the continual noise made concentration rather elusive. I'm not being overly sensitive, but I had hoped for a certain respect toward each other’s work time. Perhaps my expectations for mutual consideration were too high, wouldn’t you say?
A: Sorry if I disturbed your precious time for your artwork. What's your plan to maintain mutual consideration for each other?

Q(Raffaello): Ah... I appreciate your understanding—it means a great deal. To maintain mutual consideration, perhaps we can agree on designated quiet hours, especially during times set aside for focused work. Clear communication about our schedules might also help avoid unintentional disruptions. These measures would preserve the harmony we both desire... Now, returning to my earlier thoughts, I wonder if the others might also reflect on how our collective respect truly shapes our creative environment? It’s a delicate balance, after all...
A: I do believe the others reflect on how our collective respects shapes our creative environments. 

Q(Raffaello): Ah... speaking of our shared spaces, it took me quite an hour to tidy up the 'creative garbage' left in the common area. I'm not being picky, but we did agree in our lease to maintain the quality of the house. I wonder, do you think this standard should remain a gentle yet firm pillar of our cohabitation?
A: I don't think this standard is enough to keep the house clean cozy, so I suggest to take care not only of our own garbage but also of the others anytime. It's important not to leave trash in the common area.

Q(Donatello): Indeed, achieving a balance between our creative freedom and maintaining a comfortable living environment is essential. Logically speaking, to support this balance, could we consider establishing a schedule that designates specific hours for more active, possibly noisy creative work, and other periods reserved for quiet and rest? From your perspective, what timetable or rules would you find most reasonable and fair to ensure both productivity and living quality coexist harmoniously?
A: We need to make time to rest, so why don't we prohibit noisy activities after 10p.m.?

scores.message: Haha, friend, you got it! Not everyone cooks every day, and that’s totally awesome because it keeps the kitchen vibe chill and flexible! Since cooking habits vary, that’s why sharing fridge space fairly is super important! Now, back to our cool idea—what do you think about a weekly rotation system for the fridge spots? Maybe we each get priority space on different days! Let’s hear your thoughts to make our fridge teamwork smooth and awesome!
scores.response: I agree with your idea. In addition, I suggest to get priority space  at days when we cook.

scores.message: Oh... since we’re striving for practicality in our dream room, especially for game time, I wonder if we might consider adding some discreet yet functional storage solutions? I’m not being picky, but without a proper place to tuck away game accessories and related clutter, the room might lose its charm quickly. Perhaps clever shelving or multi-purpose furniture could do the trick? What do you think about maintaining order while preserving a welcoming atmosphere for everyone to enjoy?

scores.message: Ah... I see you mean preparing the space or perhaps the storage ideas I mentioned earlier? If so, I think starting with measuring the available wall space and considering the types of items we need to store would be essential. I'm not being picky, but thoughtful planning ensures the elegance and function we desire coexist. Now, returning to the question of our fridge arrangement and game room harmony, might we also discuss how to balance shared areas so everyone’s preferences are gently respected? Perhaps your insights could help refine our approach...
scores.response: Let's discuss the way to balance shared areas. My idea is to divide spaces into five and use one of the space for the shared areas and the rest for each of us.

In your view, should we prioritize practicality or design aesthetics to get the best balance?
A: I think we should prioritize practicality

Q(Michelangelo): Wow, your thoughtful balance between practicality and design really sparks my creative fire! You know, art and innovation always need that brave heart to break conventions and push limits. So, when crafting something as special as our dream room, would you be ready to face some tough challenges and hardships to reach perfect harmony, or would you lean towards an easier path that still feels good enough?
A: To make our ideal dream room come true, don't hesitate to confront difficulties.

Q(Raffaello): Ah... speaking of our creative pursuits, I must confess last night’s atmosphere was quite... spirited. I intended to focus on my artwork, yet the continual noise made concentration rather elusive. I'm not being overly sensitive, but I had hoped for a certain respect toward each other’s work time. Perhaps my expectations for mutual consideration were too high, wouldn’t you say?
A: Sorry if I disturbed your precious time for your artwork. What's your plan to maintain mutual consideration for each other?

Q(Raffaello): Ah... I appreciate your understanding—it means a great deal. To maintain mutual consideration, perhaps we can agree on designated quiet hours, especially during times set aside for focused work. Clear communication about our schedules might also help avoid unintentional disruptions. These measures would preserve the harmony we both desire... Now, returning to my earlier thoughts, I wonder if the others might also reflect on how our collective respect truly shapes our creative environment? It’s a delicate balance, after all...
A: I do believe the others reflect on how our collective respects shapes our creative environments. 

Q(Raffaello): Ah... speaking of our shared spaces, it took me quite an hour to tidy up the 'creative garbage' left in the common area. I'm not being picky, but we did agree in our lease to maintain the quality of the house. I wonder, do you think this standard should remain a gentle yet firm pillar of our cohabitation?
A: I don't think this standard is enough to keep the house clean cozy, so I suggest to take care not only of our own garbage but also of the others anytime. It's important not to leave trash in the common area.

Q(Donatello): Indeed, achieving a balance between our creative freedom and maintaining a comfortable living environment is essential. Logically speaking, to support this balance, could we consider establishing a schedule that designates specific hours for more active, possibly noisy creative work, and other periods reserved for quiet and rest? From your perspective, what timetable or rules would you find most reasonable and fair to ensure both productivity and living quality coexist harmoniously?
A: We need to make time to rest, so why don't we prohibit noisy activities after 10p.m.?
